title: BCI Competition 2020 Track 5 — ERP during walking (scalp, ear-EEG, and IMU)
description: This BIDS-formatted derivative dataset comprises EEG recordings from 15 healthy participants performing a visual P300 oddball task during treadmill walking at 1.6 m/s. The study includes simultaneous scalp-EEG (46 channels), ear-EEG (14 channels), electrooculography (4 channels), and inertial measurement unit recordings (6 channels) sampled at 100 Hz. Data were collected as part of BCI Competition 2020 Track 5 to evaluate brain-computer interface performance under ambulatory conditions. Trials are temporally divided into training, validation, and test sets.
methods_description: Participants performed a visual oddball P300 paradigm with target ('OOO') and non-target ('XXX') stimuli (target ratio 0.2) while walking on a treadmill at 1.6 m/s. EEG was recorded from 46 scalp channels (standard 10-20 montage) plus 14 ear-EEG channels (7 per ear), 4 EOG channels, and 6 IMU channels (3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope) at 100 Hz sampling rate. Data were epoched from -190 ms to +800 ms around stimulus onset. Trials were divided into training, validation, and test sets.
